channel.title: Rob Leggett — Blog
channel.description: Technology strategy, engineering, and AI platform insights.

item.title: Demystifying Large Language Models - Understanding the Intelligence Behind Modern AI
item.description: It feels like almost overnight, Large Language Models went from a niche topic in machine learning research to something your parents are asking about at the dinner table. Whether it is ChatGPT drafting an email, Copilot helping you write code, or a chatbot resolving your query, LLMs have woven thems

item.title: Building a Local AI Playground - Classical ML Meets Modern LLMs
item.description: If I'm to be perfectly honest, the best way I have found to genuinely understand a technology is to build something end-to-end with it — not a toy example from a tutorial, but a project that forces you to make real architectural decisions and deal with the friction that only surfaces when you integr

item.title: Azure Multi-Subscription Architecture - Enterprise Landing Zones
item.description: If I'm to be perfectly honest, Azure's approach to enterprise-scale cloud architecture is the most prescriptive of the three major cloud providers — and I mean that as a compliment. Where AWS and GCP give you the building blocks and leave you to design the structure, Microsoft has invested heavily i

item.title: Infrastructure as Code with AWS CDK - Building Reusable Constructs at Scale
item.description: For those who know me, you will know that I have strong opinions about infrastructure. I have always believed that if you cannot reproduce your entire cloud environment from a single command, you do not truly own it. Over the years I have worked with CloudFormation templates that stretched into thou

item.title: GCP Multi-Project Architecture - Organising Cloud at Scale
item.description: It feels like every conversation I have about cloud architecture eventually arrives at the same fundamental question — how do you organise things at scale? Whether you are managing ten projects or ten thousand, the answer is never "just create another project and figure it out later." If you have wo

item.title: AWS Multi-Account Architecture - Scaling Cloud with Account Isolation
item.description: For those who know me, you will know that I have spent a significant amount of time working with AWS across large enterprise environments. item.title: Building AI-Powered Applications in the Cloud - A Practical Guide for Engineers

item.title: The Rise of AI Agents - Autonomous Systems That Think and Act

item.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) - Grounding AI in Your Enterprise Data

item.title: Responsible AI - Building Trust Through Ethical and Transparent Systems

item.title: AI at the Edge - Bringing Intelligence Closer to Where It Matters

item.title: Prompt Engineering - The Art and Science of Communicating with AI
item.description: For those who know me, you will know that I am always looking for ways to work smarter and more effectively with the tools at our disposal. Over the past couple of years, large language models (LLMs) have fundamentally changed how we interact with technology — from writing code to analysing document

item.title: The AI-Ready Organisation - Preparing Your Teams and Culture for an AI-Driven Future
item.description: For those who know me, you will know that I have always believed technology transformation is fundamentally a people challenge, not a technology one. I have seen firsthand how organisations with average technology but exceptional culture outperform those with cutting-edge tools but poor alignment. T

item.title: Fine-Tuning vs Foundation Models - Choosing the Right Approach for Your AI Strategy

One of the most consequential decisions organisations face in their AI journey today is this: should we use a f

item.title: AI-Powered Developer Tools - How AI is Transforming the Way We Build Software
item.description: For those who know me, you will know that I have always been passionate about developer experience and the tools that make engineering teams more productive. Throughout my career, I have seen paradigm shifts — from manual deployments to CI/CD, from monoliths to microservices, from on-premises to clo

item.title: Deep Dive - Real-Time Computer Vision Platforms

item.title: Deep Dive - Data Mesh Architecture

item.description: In today's digital age, managing digital identities efficiently and securely has become a cornerstone for any online platform. item.description: In the digital landscape, maintaining a secure and reliable online presence is paramount. item.title: Unleashing Potential - How Good Leadership Fosters Growth in Exceptional Individuals
item.description: Leadership comes in many styles. Some good, and some well, let's just say not so good. item.title: Navigating the Multicloud Maze - Simplifying Service Names Across Cloud Providers
item.description: Working with multiple cloud providers can be a complex endeavor, and one of the unexpected challenges is keeping track of service names and their equivalents across different platforms. However, fret not, for we have a solution that can simplify your multicloud journey. In this article, we present h

item.title: How I passed my TOGAF 9.2 Exam

item.title: Embarking on the Cloud Certification Journey - Uniquely Personal and Profound

As I've witnessed numerous individuals undertaking their own certification journeys, I've come to realize that each story is deeply personal and incredibly diverse. item.title: Embracing the Cloud - Understanding the Fundamentals of Cloud Technology
item.description: Working in the IT industry, many of my friends and family approach me when it comes to anything "computer" related, due to them having very little knowledge about IT in general with one of the most mysterious topics for them being the Cloud. They often hear terms like, "it is in the cloud", or they

item.title: How I passed my AWS Certified Security (Speciality) Exam

item.title: Choosing Event Streaming or Messaging for your Architecture

item.title: Why I don’t think Googles Data Loss Prevention API is ready for use with images
item.description: Data protection is critical within any business and data has a lot of value. Loss of data can be detrimental to both the business holding the data and the individual who's data has been compromised resulting in drastic consequences. Loss of data is a breach of both privacy and security and with data

item.title: Choosing Choreography or Orchestration for your Microservices Architecture

Just because you have taken one of these approaches in a previo

item.title: How I passed the AWS Certified Solution Architect (Associate) Exam

item.title: Unlocking the Power of Component Libraries - A Comprehensive Exploration
item.description: Within the vast realm of software development, numerous companies and individuals have contributed remarkable component libraries, generously shared and readily available on GitHub. item.title: Publishing a static site with Github Pages and Travis CI
item.description: When trying to find instructions on how to publish into Github Pages, I found that is was not very clear when trying to publish a static site. I thought it would be good to write a guide on publishing a static site into Github Pages and hopefully eliminate any confusion in trying to do so. --- Befo

item.title: Achieving Excellence - Understanding High Performance in Agile Teams
item.description: Do you enjoy your work culture? Who doesn't want to show up to work and be in an environment with like-minded and talented team members (ability, passion, desire not skill-set). Do you enjoy your work environment? Who doesn't want to be part of a team that is recognised for the quality that they del

item.title: Secure WebSockets with Spring Boot 3 and Spring Security 6 - Token-Based Authentication
item.description: WebSockets with custom message security is not a topic that appears widely online, let alone example on how to make it happen, therefore I decided to present on web sockets and in particular allowing custom security on the socket level. This project has been fully modernised from its original Angula

item.title: Jersey 4 RESTful Web Service - Modern Jakarta EE with JPA and Derby

item.title: Agile Empowerment - Realizing the Benefits of Agile in Information Technology
item.description: In today's post, we will explore the remarkable success of Agile methodology in the realm of Information Technology and Business. item.title: Angular 19 and Bootstrap 5 with Spring Boot 3 and Spring Security 6
item.description: Folks have been asking me if I knew of any good examples of a modern full-stack web application that utilises the following technologies: Angular 19, Bootstrap 5.3, Spring Boot 3.3.7, Spring Security 6, HSQLDB, and JWT stateless authentication, and after searching around I decided that it would be b
